x-artifact.note: Design-first artifact only — describes the MCP tool surface, not a running server. Tool input/output schemas $ref the same object schemas as the OpenAPI; each tool maps to an OpenAPI operationId. Surfaces the Staten Island Borough President's charter functions (land-use recommendations, community board appointments, discretionary funding, borough board resolutions, community events, BP Assist) that have no API today, and adds two net-new write workflows: report_quality_of_life_issue (BP Assist) and apply_to_community_board.

server.name: io.nyc.statenislandbp
server.title: Staten Island Borough President
server.description: Agent-native access to the Office of the Staten Island Borough President — land-use (ULURP) recommendations, community board appointments, discretionary capital/expense funding awards, borough board resolutions, and the community calendar, none of which have an API or a real Open Data source today, plus the net-new ability to report a quality-of-life issue to the BP Assist help line and to apply to serve on a community board.
server.instructions: This office has almost no machine-readable source — a Weebly brochure site and two trivial datasets — so most tools describe a proposed contract over data that today exists only as PDFs and HTML. find_land_use_recommendations / get_land_use_recommendation cover the BP's ULURP review role; find_community_board_appointments lists the three Staten Island boards' membership; find_discretionary_funding_awards covers capital/expense allocations; find_resolutions covers Borough Board resolutions and reports; find_events covers the community calendar and Sounds of Summer concerts. find_constituent_requests reads BP Assist data — remember the only existing dataset is AGGREGATE counts by request type, not individual requests. Two tools MUTATE state, so confirm details before submitting: report_quality_of_life_issue files a BP Assist request (potholes, litter, graffiti) and apply_to_community_board (net-new) submits a community board membership application — the applicant must be a NYC resident of, or have a significant interest in, the district. NOTE: all five NYC borough-president offices are near-identical; this same tool surface should federate into one shared Borough President server keyed by borough.

tools.name: find_land_use_recommendations
tools.title: Find Land-Use Recommendations
tools.description: Search Borough President recommendations on ULURP / land-use applications by community board, application type, disposition, or period.

tools.name: get_land_use_recommendation
tools.title: Get Land-Use Recommendation
tools.description: Fetch a single Borough President land-use recommendation by its ULURP / City Planning application number.

tools.name: find_community_board_appointments
tools.title: Find Community Board Appointments
tools.description: List members / appointments across Staten Island's three community boards, filtered by board, role, or status.

tools.name: find_discretionary_funding_awards
tools.title: Find Discretionary Funding Awards
tools.description: Search the Borough President's discretionary capital and expense funding awards by fiscal year, funding type, or recipient type.

tools.name: find_resolutions
tools.title: Find Resolutions
tools.description: List Staten Island Borough Board resolutions, reports, and minutes by type or period.

tools.name: find_events
tools.title: Find Events
tools.description: List Staten Island community events and Sounds of Summer concerts by category or date range.

tools.name: find_constituent_requests
tools.title: Find Constituent Requests
tools.description: Read BP Assist help line quality-of-life requests. The only existing Open Data (3fes-huds) is AGGREGATE annual counts by request type — set aggregate=true for those; individual requests have no public source today.

tools.name: report_quality_of_life_issue
tools.title: Report a Quality-of-Life Issue (BP Assist)
tools.description: File a BP Assist help line request (pothole, litter, graffiti, and similar) to be relayed to the relevant city agency. NET-NEW write workflow with no API today. Mutates state — confirm the issue type, description, and location with the user before submitting.

tools.name: apply_to_community_board
tools.title: Apply to a Community Board
tools.description: Submit a Staten Island community board membership application — the NET-NEW write surface that today is only an opaque Weebly form. Mutates state. The applicant must be a NYC resident of, or have a significant interest in, the district; confirm the applicant details, requested board, and residency before submitting.
